preamble: The shift from the alveolar fricative [s] to a glottal stop [ʔ] is an extreme form of debuccalization. It typically represents the final stage of lenition where [s] first aspirates to [h] before losing its oral friction entirely, leaving only a glottal catch. This is a characteristic feature of certain Lowland Spanish dialects.

examples.note: In some dialects, the aspiration of syllable-final /s/ is realized as a weak glottal stop.
